message: Empty-result human output ignores non-default --by and labels the table as STAGE.
evidence: The selected grouping is only stored on groups; when groups is empty, writeUsageSummaryHuman defaults by to "stage", so empty or missing-ledger summaries with --by provider/model/agent display the wrong table header.

message: Human output falls back to STAGE for empty summaries even when another --by value was selected.
evidence: buildUsageSummary only preserves the selected grouping inside group rows; writeUsageSummaryHuman defaults to "stage" when len(r.Groups)==0, so empty or missing-ledger summaries cannot render the chosen --by header.

message: TestUsageSummaryNoWorkspaceMutation does not actually detect file modifications. It snapshots only relative paths, so a pactum usage implementation that rewrites an existing file would still pass.
evidence: workspaceSnapshot appends only filepath.Rel results and returns strings.Join(files), while the contract requires verifying no create, modify, or delete during pactum usage.

message: The focused usage tests do not exercise pactum usage with an omitted run_id, so the current-run default and no-current failure path required by the contract are untested for this command.
evidence: All usage command invocations in usage_test.go pass either an explicit runID or --all; existing omitted-run tests cover contract show, not the new usage command behavior.

message: The --by agent human-label fallback is untested. The only --by agent test records both set AgentName, so the required fallback to agent when agent_name is empty is not covered.
evidence: TestUsageSummaryByDimensions sets AgentName on both records and only asserts those names appear in human output, leaving the empty-agent_name fallback branch untested.

message: Empty usage summaries silently render the wrong grouping header because the human renderer falls back to stage when there are no groups.
evidence: writeUsageSummaryHuman derives the header from r.Groups[0].By and defaults to stage when Groups is empty; missing/empty ledgers produce empty Groups.

message: The usage documentation still describes the removed --top/by_run/effective_units/cache-ratio usage output instead of the new --by-based pactum.usage_summary.v1alpha1 view.
evidence: docs/cost-budget-design.md lines 296-309 document --top N, by_run sorting, cache_read_ratio, by-attempt, and effective_units; internal/app/cli.go lines 334-338 now expose --by and no --top, and internal/app/usage.go lines 160-166 define the new usage_summary schema without those fields.
